A FEW POPLAR TREES AND SEVERAL SMALL BRANCHES KNOCKED DOWN FROM BLACK RIVER HARBOR TO IRONWOOD AND WAKEFIELD. CONCURRENT WIND GUST OF 46MPH RECORDED AT KIWD. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

TREES DOWN ACROSS KUUSISTO RD AND HIDDEN ACRES ROAD. JCBM4 REPORTED GUSTS OF 50 MPH BETWEEN 545 PM AND 6 PM EDT. HOUGHTON COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER ALSO REPORTED TREES DOWN AND POWER OUTAGES ACROSS THE COUNTY.
FAIRLY LARGE TREE DOWN ON POWERLINES AT PELKIE RD AND M-38. REPORTED BY DISPATCH AND SHERIFFS OFFICE. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR AND TIME OF REPORT TO SHERIFF.
GRANITE ISLAND WIND GUST
HAIL FELL FOR SEVERAL MINUTES. MIXTURE OF QUARTER-PEA AND PENNY SIZED HAIL. VERY HEAVY RAIN AND GUSTY WINDS.
MEASURED AT THE ROCK OF AGES LIGHT HOUSE.
ROOF TORN OFF SHED. MULTIPLE POWER POLES DOWN ACROSS THE TOWN.
6 INCH DIAMETER TREE DOWN. OTHER TREES BENDING CONSIDERABLY.
6-8 INCH DIAMETER TREES DOWN BLOCKING COUNTY ROAD 407 TO THE WEST OF MUSKALLONGE LAKE STATE PARK.
8 TO 10 TREES DOWN AT MUSKALLONGE LAKE STATE PARK. SOME TREES FELL ON CAMPERS AND TRUCKS. NO INJURIES.
TREE DOWN BLOCKING PART OF M-35 NEAR NEGAUNEE JUST SOUTH OF US-41
TREES DOWNED ALONG AND NEAR CR-407. TIME IS ESTIMATED PUBLIC WENT BY THIS AREA AROUND 2 AM 19 JULY AND SAW TREES DOWNED NEAR ROAD.
TREES DOWN...ONE OF WHICH DAMAGED HOUSE DECK.
GUST MEASURED AT THE MICHIGAN TECH BUOY.
WIND GUST OVER 60 MPH NEAR KEARSARGE. REPORTS OF TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN ACROSS KEWEENAW ON 800 MHZ RADIO.
PEAK GUST AT THE GRANITE ISLAND LIGHT STATION.
ESCANABA PUBLIC SAFETY REPORTED WIDESPREAD TREES AND POWER LINES DOWNED IN DELTA COUNTY. SOME DOWNED IN ESCANABA, GLADSTONE AND RAPID RIVER.
SEVERAL TREES REPORTED DOWN AROUND THE FELCH AREA.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN OR SNAPPED.
LARGE TREE BRANCHES REPORTED DOWN. NO ESTIMATE OF SIZE OR NUMBER. COUNTY DISPATCH REPORTS A METAL UTILITY SHED WAS BLOWN OUT OF A YARD.
AT MENOMINEE MARINE NOS SITE.
3 TREES OF UNKNOWN DIAMETER BLOWN DOWN IN MENOMINEE. SEVERAL SMALL LIMBS WERE ALSO BLOWN DOWN.
QUARTER TO DIME SIZED HAIL FELL FOR ABOUT 5 TO 10 MINS.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN. ESTIMATED ONE OF THE TREES TO HAVE A 12-15 INCH DIAMETER. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
MOSTLY QUARTER SIZED HAIL. ALSO MENTIONED THERE WERE A FEW FLAT HAIL STONES THAT HAD THE DIAMETER OF A GOLF BALL.
SOME QUARTER SIZED HAIL WITH A LOT OF PEA TO DIME SIZED HAIL. LITTLE WIND.
QUARTER SIZED HAIL.
TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR. REPORT VIA WLUC TV FACEBOOK PAGE.
SEVERAL REPORTS OF DIME AND QUARTER SIZED HAIL.
HAD TWO DIFFERENT STORMS THAT WENT THROUGH AND BOTH DROPPED QUARTER SIZED HAIL. FIRST STORM WENT THROUGH AROUND 1805 EDT...THE SECOND CAME THROUGH AROUND 1823 EDT.
FOOT DIAMETER PINE TREE DOWN ACROSS WAWONOWIN CLUB ROAD. VIA TV6. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
RELAYED THROUGH TV6 NEWS AND SOCIAL MEDIA. NUMEROUS TREES DOWN AROUND ISHPEMING...ESPECIALLY WEST AND SOUTH OF TOWN. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

ISOLATED TREE DAMAGE AROUND THE AREA OF WEST ISHPEMING. MOST TREE DAMAGE IN THE IMMEDIATE AREA WAS NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF RANDALL/GREENWOOD/HOLLYHOCK STREETS. LARGEST TREE WAS 10-12 INCHES PINE TREE SNAPPED NEAR BASE. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
2 TREES ROUGHLY A FOOT IN DIAMETER FELL NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF CR-478 AND M28 WEST OF ISHPEMING. ONE TREE CLIPPED THE CORNER OF A MOBILE HOME AND ANOTHER FELL ON A CAMPER TRAILER AND TRUCK.

A FEW TREES UPROOTED OR SNAPPED AND BRACHES BROKEN OFF IN THE SOUTH ANGELINE/SALISBURY/TERRACE STREET AREA SOUTH OF ISHPEMING. ONE LARGE BRANCH FELL ON A CAR...BREAKING THE BACK WINDOW AND DOING ADDITIONAL BODY DAMAGE.
LARGE TREE DOWN AT INTERSECTION OF M35 AND COUNTY ROAD MP. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
PART OF LARGE TREE FELL ONTO WRIGHT STREET NEAR FITCH AVENUE IN MARQUETTE AND NARROWLY MISSED A HOUSE.

LUCE COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS TREES DOWN ACROSS NORTHERN LUCE COUNTY. MORE SPECIFICALLY...AT THE BLIND SUCKER CAMPGROUND WHERE A TREE FELL ON A TENT...AND AT MUSKALLONGE STATE PARK WHERE POWER LINES WERE DOWN AS WELL. NO KNOWN INJURIES.
TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR. RELAYED FROM PMO.
REPORT VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
